---
title: Git workflow
---

Once a repository has been cloned and GitHub access is configured, most
updates require only a small set of Git commands. Run them from the repository
root.

## Before editing: get current changes

Update your local copy before starting work:

```bash
git pull
```

If this command reports a conflict or asks for authentication, stop and
resolve that issue before editing. See [Setting up Git](../getting_started/git.qmd)
for additional guidance.

## After editing: see what changed

Check which files have been changed:

```bash
git status
```

Optionally inspect the actual text changes:

```bash
git diff
```

You normally want to commit edited `.qmd` source documents and any deliberate
figures, datasets, or configuration changes. Avoid committing locally
generated website output unless the repository explicitly requires it.

## Send your update to GitHub

Add only the files belonging to your update:

```bash
git add path/to/lesson.qmd
```

Record the update with a short message:

```bash
git commit -m "Clarify exercise instructions"
```

Push the commit to the remote repository:

```bash
git push
```

## Check the published result

In repositories published through GitHub Actions, pushing usually triggers a
new render and deployment. After pushing:

1. Check the repository's **Actions** tab on GitHub.
2. Confirm that the build completed successfully.
3. Open the published site or output and check the updated page.

## Command summary

```bash
git pull
# edit and preview the course material
git status
git diff
git add path/to/lesson.qmd
git commit -m "Describe the change"
git push
```
